message:
test(lifecycle): diag connector entries uniform shape (ibuuh.19)

ibuuh.19 connector-inventory contract row. cass diag --json reports
per-connector detection status as an array of {name, path, found}
objects. Every entry must have all three keys with the expected types
(string, string, bool) regardless of which detector fires — a missing
or mis-typed field in one entry silently skews retention / GC logic
that enumerates connector outputs.

New test diag_connector_entries_have_uniform_shape walks the
connectors array against an isolated HOME. For each entry asserts:
 * entry is an object
 * name is a non-empty string
 * path is a non-empty string ('(not detected)' when absent is OK)
 * found is a bool

Intentionally does NOT assert all-false — some connectors (aider)
scan CWD in addition to HOME, so discovered CWD hits are a real
possibility under cargo test. The shape/type invariants are the
stable part the retention contract depends on; agents enumerating
connectors must be able to trust every entry's field types.

VERIFICATION:
cargo test --test lifecycle_matrix diag_connector: 2/2 pass (this
row + the pre-existing capabilities/diag enumerate-same-set row).

Follow-up slice on coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.19.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
